message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>We hypothesized that reported SARS-CoV-2 infection numbers are underestimated and piloted a point prevalence by rapid antigen testing in the VACCELERATE volunteer registry.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>Between July-1 and July-7, 2022, 7/419 (1.67%) tests were positive. Compared to reports of the German Federal Government, our results suggest a 2.39-fold higher prevalence.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>Our findings imply that the actual prevalence of SARS-CoV-2 may be higher than detected by current surveillance systems, so that current pandemic surveillance and testing strategies need to be adapted.</jats:p>
title:
A pilot surveillance report of SARS-CoV-2 rapid antigen test results among volunteers in Germany, 1
                  <sup>st</sup>
                  week of July 2022
